samples.xml_description:
<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for pulse detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </input>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="S0">Initial state of the pulse detection process.</enum>
        <enum id="S1">State indicating that a pulse is detected.</enum>
        <enum id="S2">State indicating that a pulse is detected.</enum>
        <enum id="S3">State indicating the end of a pulse.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="data_out_reg">Store the output signal data_out.</sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module pulse_detect detects pulses in the input signal data_in. A pulse is defined as a sequence of 1s and 0s, specifically 010, which triggers the output signal data_out to 1. The module operates in a state machine with four states: S0, S1, S2, and S3. 
    In the state S0, the module waits for data_in to become 1. Once data_in becomes 1, it transitions to state S1. If data_in becomes 0, it returns to state S0. In state S1, the module waits for data_in to become 0, transitioning to state S2. In state S2, it waits for data_in to become 1, transitioning to state S3. In state S3, the module sets the output signal data_out to 1 and transitions back to state S1. The module resets to state S0 when rst_n is asserted.
    </implementation>
</module>
